The worst thing is that I see would-be well-intentioned allies caught up in the froth of an outrage mob attacking a person who they just don't know very well. It's crazy to see you eating your own like this. And it all happened so quickly.

The thing that worries me about this Quinn Norton thing is that a decade of doing hard work, being ideologically consistent, and standing behind your personal convictions can have the Twitter judge, jury, and executioner throw a wrench in your career over their misunderstanding.

anyway, all of those people spun out tons of media, photography, videos, and vibes that had a sense of "here's what we've figured out so far, here's a romantic aesthetic" – and I kind of feel like what we've left the zoomers is "ALL FUCKED, GOOD LUCK"

From my perspective, as a teenager in 2007, the center of gravity of all culture was Williamsburg, Brooklyn and the people who lived in it- this is where people went to be creative, try new things, be weird, and succeed at it

I was 15 years old in 2007 when Tumblr was created, along with gems like https://t.co/nZUEmxPPmq, Vice's early online video platform where I could watch epicly later'd and weird gonzo documentaries and try and figure out the world

pretty sure the millenials are failing the zoomers right now- gen x gave us all sorts of blogs and vimeo videos and terrible street art explaining how to be in the world, but all WE are giving the kids are algorithmically-mutated 15 seconds tik toks and hustler twitter threads
